title: Define Tree Properties
text: Define Tree Properties Specify the structure and properties of a tree widget. Use this dialog box to define the initial tree structure. To populate the tree with data at run time, you must define a ValRep list in the exec operation of the form on which this widget is painted. Buttons Columns —define the columns and properties in the list view. Types —define tree item types in the tree. Structure —define the location of tree items in the tree hierarchy. Specify the Build Tree Structure By property first. More —specify additional properties for this widget. Properties Tree View and List View Collapse/Expand Buttons (HasButtons) Node Lines (HasLines) Root Lines (HasRootLines) Auto Expand (AutoExpand) Selection Delay ( SelectionDelay) List View Style (ListView) Show Header ( Header) Allow Sorting MultiSelect ( ListMultiSelect) Build Tree Structure By Width of Tree Separation String Frame 3-D Effect (3d) Inline Edit ( InlineEdit) Attach to Window Border (attach) Background Color (BackColor) Foreground Color (ForeColor) Widget Font ( Font) Label Font ( LabelFont) How to Defining Tree Widgets Defining and Updating ValRep Lists for the Tree Widget Dynamically Building the Tree Structure Related Links utree (Tree) The utree widget enables users to view and navigate structured data as a hierarchy. Uniface provides a logical widget called Tree, which is mapped to utree. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
